scope_note: QA Langlands Ramified Prime p=5 Euler Factor cert [411]. Primary sources: Bushnell, C.J. and Henniart, G. (2006) 'The Local Langlands Conjecture for GL(2)' doi:10.1007/978-3-540-31511-7 (§14: conductor exponent n≥2 implies supercuspidal for GL₂ over a p-adic field; supercuspidal reps have trivial local L-factor); Arthur, J. and Clozel, L. (1989) 'Simple Algebras, Base Change, and the Advanced Theory of the Trace Formula' ISBN 978-0-691-08517-3 (Artin conductor formula for automorphic induction, conductor of induced reps). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a (GL₂/F, F=Q(sqrt5), CM by K=Q(zeta_5)) with conductor level 125=5^3 (so conductor ideal P_5^3 at the unique prime P_5 above 5 in F), the GL₄/Q automorphic induction AI(f) has trivial local Euler factor at p=5: P_5^{ram}(Y)=1 (degree 0, the unit polynomial). Derivation (3 steps): (1) RAMIFIED CLASSIFICATION: p=5 satisfies 5%5=0, distinct from split (p%5 in {1,4}) and inert (p%5 in {2,3}). Together {0}∪{1,4}∪{2,3}={0,1,2,3,4} covers all residues mod 5, so [404]+[409]+[411] is the complete unramified+ramified GL₄/Q AI Euler product classification. (2) SUPERCUSPIDAL TYPE: The conductor exponent of f at P_5 is n=3. For GL₂ over a p-adic local field (Bushnell-Henniart §14): n=0→unramified; n=1→Steinberg/special; n≥2→supercuspidal. Since n=3≥2, the local component π_{P_5} of f is supercuspidal. (3) TRIVIAL EULER FACTOR: Supercuspidal representations have trivial local L-factor L(s,π_{P_5})=1 (no Hecke eigenvalue, no root of L-poly). Under automorphic induction, Ind_{W_{F_5}}^{W_{Q_5}}(ρ_{P_5}) has no Frobenius-fixed vectors in the inertia coinvariants, giving P_5^{ram}(Y)=1 for AI(f). CONDUCTOR EXPONENT (C4): By the Artin conductor formula for induced representations, a_5(AI(f)) = [F_5:Q_5]·n + dim(ρ_f)·f(F_5/Q_5) = 2·3 + 2·1 = 8. Here [F_5:Q_5]=2 (tamely ramified quadratic), n=3, dim(ρ_f)=2, f(F_5/Q_5)=1 (discriminant exponent of tame quadratic Q_5(sqrt5)/Q_5). ALL computations are integer (Theorem NT: no float observer projections enter QA state). Four checks, p=5: (C1) p%5=0, partition {0}∪{1,4}∪{2,3} complete and disjoint (PASS); (C2) n=3≥2, supercuspidal confirmed, n≠0 unramified, n≠1 Steinberg (PASS); (C3) P_5^{ram}=[1], degree 0, degree<4=split/inert degree (PASS); (C4) 2·3+2·1=8, all inputs int (PASS). With [410] (Dedekind zeta), [404]+[409]+[410]+[411] encodes the complete ζ_{Q(sqrt5)} prime-splitting and GL₄/Q AI Euler product structure. Derived 2026-06-13.
